Creating a Windows Install USB From macOS
Had to reinstall Windows on a machine at home, but my main machine is a Mac. So here's how to make a Windows install USB from macOS.
Grab the Windows ISO from Microsoft's official site. I'll use Windows 10 here.
This post doesn't provide a Windows license key.
ISO is a disc image format — basically a single-file archive of a disc's contents. Burning software writes the ISO contents to a disc (or in our case, a USB).
You'll need a USB drive of at least 64GB. Before anything else, format it.
Formatting ensures the USB is clean, so the Windows install gets written correctly.
Insert the USB. You should see the drive on your desktop. Right-click and pick "Erase".
In the erase dialog, pick "ExFAT" as the format and click "Erase".
MS-DOS is the older format. ExFAT is what you want.
Double-click the downloaded Windows 10 ISO. macOS automatically mounts it as a virtual disc, and you'll see it on your desktop.
Open it and you'll see the Windows 10 install files.
Open a terminal (I'm using iTerm2) and run:
language-bash shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast
This lists all disks so you can find the USB's name. Mine's called "UFDRecovery".
Now copy the Windows files to the USB:
Hit Enter and the Windows 10 install files start copying to the USB. Takes a moment.
When done, it looks like the above. (Note: there's no completion prompt — when it returns, it's done.)
is recursive. So this copies the Windows 10 install files recursively from
